entities.name: NITROGEN TRANSFORMATIONS
entities.description: The overall process of nitrogen cycling including mineralization, nitrification, and nitrous oxide production

entities.name: MINERALIZATION
entities.description: The process of converting organic nitrogen to inorganic forms

entities.name: NITRIFICATION
entities.description: The biological oxidation of ammonia to nitrite and then to nitrate

entities.name: NITROUS OXIDE PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of generating N2O gas from nitrogen transformations

entities.name: ACID FOREST FLOOR SOILS
entities.description: Soils collected from climax forest sites characterized by low pH

entities.name: BLACKHAWK ISLAND
entities.description: The specific site in Wisconsin where soil samples were collected

entities.name: WISCONSIN
entities.description: The US state where the study sites are located

entities.name: SOIL ACIDITY
entities.description: The pH level of the soil, ranging from 3.9 to 5.1

entities.name: ORGANIC MATTER CONCENTRATIONS
entities.description: The percentage of organic matter in the soil, ranging from 2.4 to 59.0 percent

entities.name: AEROBIC INCUBATION
entities.description: The process of incubating soil samples aerobically for 4 weeks at 30°C

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A chemical treatment added to the soil as KH2PO4

entities.name: CALCIUM CARBONATE
entities.description: A chemical treatment used for liming the soil

entities.name: AMMONIFICATION
entities.description: The process of converting organic nitrogen into ammonia

entities.name: SM ALFISOL
entities.description: A specific type of soil where nitrification was nearly complete

entities.name: HISTOSOL
entities.description: A specific type of soil where nitrification was almost nonexistent

entities.name: NITROUS OXIDE EMISSION RATES
entities.description: The rate at which N2O is released from the soil
